summary: Re-verified iter_16 track-link fix. File on disk (lines 386-388) contains the unconditional `body_lines[4] = f'Track live: {track_url}'` assignment. Re-ran iter_15 suite and full regression under RESTRICT_OUTBOUND_TO_PHONE=+918200663263. All targeted tests pass; iter_15 went 9/12 (3 acceptable skips for fully-shipped fixture) and full regression 93 passed / 5 skipped / 0 failed across iter9-iter15.
critical_code_review_comments: universal_update.py fix is clean: unconditional assignment to body_lines[4] with padding-to-5 guarantees the Meta 5-var template is respected regardless of preset headline length.
updated_files: /app/backend/.env (temporary during test; restored to blank RESTRICT_OUTBOUND_TO_PHONE at teardown)
test_credentials: admin@hrexporter.com / Admin@123 (not needed — tests operate on service layer directly)
context_for_next_testing_agent: iter_16 report was stale — the track-link fix in /app/backend/services/universal_update.py lines 386-388 is confirmed working. iter_15 suite: 9 passed / 3 skipped (skips are fixture-limited, not bugs). Full regression iter9-iter15: 93/98 passing, remaining 5 are pre-existing acceptable skips. RESTRICT_OUTBOUND_TO_PHONE restored to blank + backend restarted at teardown.
rca of the issue: iter_16 falsely reported the fix missing. Fresh `view_file` in iter_17 confirmed lines 386-388 contain the correct unconditional assignment. Running the exact same test suite against the current on-disk file yields the previously-failing TestAutoSendPresetAppendsTrackLink::test_track_url_encoded_order_number_appended as PASSED. No further action required.
